fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but currently unprofitable with a high forward valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate with a surprise of 174.6%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 224.8% — significant future growth expected.
Strong buy consensus from 15 analysts with a mean target of $80.40.
Current ratio 7.18 and Quick ratio 6.51 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and Profit margin -20.5% — currently loss-making.
Return on equity -4.2% and Return on assets -2.1% — inefficient capital use.
Forward P/E 58.30 and Price/Sales (TTM) 34.55 — very high valuation multiples.
Beta 1.91 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: MP Materials stock has seen pressure despite a recent partnership with Apple, with articles citing geopolitical factors, insider sales, and shifting expectations as reasons for the stock's recent decline. The company's valuation is under scrutiny, though analysts maintain a strong buy consensus with a mean target of $80.40. The stock's trading has been described as strange, with recent price action deviating from its historical performance.

headlines.title: Despite Its Recent Partnership with Apple, MP Materials Stock Is Down. Here's Why.
